Shikhar Bharadwaj

Research student at IISc (Intelligent Systems Lab)

Email: shikharb@iisc.ac.in

Phone: 7989962442

About Me

Hi, I am a research student at IISc, Bangalore. I am interested in Machine Learning, NLP, Computational Geometry and Algorithms. I do competitive programming for fun.

Projects

API for Computational Geometry Algorithms

Developed an API in C++ for generating Convex Hulls and Triangulations and Delaunay triangulation of Input Polygons.

GPU Accelarated Range Data Structures

Implemented Range Tree for Orthogonal Range Query problem in CUDA.Compared efficiency with CPU implementation for the same.

Experience

Media Net

media.net

Software Development Engineer

Aug 2018-July 2019

◦Ad Retrieval: Implemented OkapiBM25F algorithm for retrieving text ads based on search keywords. Modified the algo to use word2vec embeddings.

◦ Text Clustering: Implemented K-Means, Affinity Propagation and Hierarchical clustering on search keywords for grouping user intent. Used Hive for creating data aggregation pipeline. Also developed a UI for reviewing clusters (CRUD operations)

◦ Constrained AB Testing: Devised and coded an algorithm to compare performance of ad-retrieval algorithms.

◦ Redis Cluster Migration: Migrated twemproxy redis cluster to redis cluster to achieve better cluster management and efficient use of resources.

◦ Redis Replicator: Maintained an application to replicate data from database to redis. Added checkpointing algorithm to the app to ensure data consistency.

◦ Training Project-Custom Site Search: Used Elasticsearch to index websites for customised searching. Also used Redis as a message queue for connecting with frontend.

Microsoft Research

Research Intern

Dec 2017-May 2018

◦ ActorDB: The project involves contribution to Microsofts P family of programming languages. These are domain-specific language for designing safe and robust asynchronous as well as distributed systems. Used P3(C++ based) to develop a scalable key value store. Also improved the testing framework for P3.

Indira Gandhi Center for Atomic Research

Research Intern

May - July 2016

◦ Search Engine: Worked in Information Processing Division on a project to compare efficiency of Information Retrieval methods using TF-IDF vectorization and Fuzzy Retrieval. Also developed a GUI for the same using Python.

Publications

An Extraction Based approach to Keyword generation and Precedence Retrieval

http://ceur-ws.org/Vol-2036/T3-4.pdf

Precedence Retrieval involves ranking documents according to their importance for a given query. Developed an algorithm to perform keyword based summarization of document and then used thse summaries to rank documents. Published in Working Notes of Forum for Information Retrieval Evaluation (FIRE) 2017.
